title: Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 599.
summary: This act is a U.S. government appropriations bill for the Department of the Interior and environment-related agencies in fiscal year 2027. It likely outlines funding and policy decisions for these agencies.

title: Carnivals are Real Entertainment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Carnivals are Real Entertainment Act appears to be a law that acknowledges and supports the value of carnivals as a type of legitimate entertainment. The exact specifics of the policy are unclear due to a lack of detailed information.

title: Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 605.
summary: The Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2027 outlines federal funding allocation for the Department of Homeland Security to support its operations and initiatives. This act aims to provide the necessary resources to ensure the security and resilience of the United States.

title: Next-Generation Geothermal Research and Development Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: The Next-Generation Geothermal Research and Development Act is a policy that aims to support and fund research into next-generation geothermal technology. This policy likely aims to increase the availability of geothermal energy and provide a cleaner alternative to traditional fossil fuels.

title: To amend title 49, United States Code, to grant the United States Virgin Islands and Puerto Rico eligibility to issue commercial driver's licenses, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Highways and Transit.
summary: This policy aims to allow Puerto Rico and the United States Virgin Islands to issue commercial driver's licenses. The change is part of a broader update to US law.

title: Natural GAS Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Natural GAS Act of 2025 is a proposed legislation that likely aims to regulate or manage natural gas resources. The exact specifics and goals of the act are not specified.

title: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agency Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Received in the Senate.
summary: This act is an appropriations bill that allocates funds to various government agencies involved in agriculture, rural development, and food and drug regulation. It aims to support these sectors through funding allocations as part of the 2027 federal budget.

title: To permanently extend the exemption from the engine compartment portion of the pre-trip vehicle inspection skills testing requirement for school bus drivers, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Highways and Transit.
summary: This policy aims to prolong a specific exemption from pre-trip inspections for school bus drivers. It appears to be related to vehicle safety regulations and the qualifications of school bus drivers.

title: To amend the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the transport, purchase, and sale of pelts of, and handicrafts, garments, and art produced from, Southcentral and Southeast Alaska northern sea otters that are taken for subsistence purposes.
action: Subcommittee Hearings Held
summary: This policy amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act to allow the sale and trade of certain Alaska northern sea otter products for subsistence purposes. It specifically focuses on the transport, purchase, and sale of items like pelts and handicrafts made from these otters in specific Alaska regions.

title: ARTIST Act
action: Signed by President.
summary: The ARTIST Act is a policy document with no provided description, but its title suggests it may relate to supporting artists or creatives through the use of advanced technologies and regulations.

title: Highway Funding Transferability Improvement Act
action: Committee on Environment and Public Works Senate Subcommittee on Transportation and Infrastructure. Hearings held.
summary: The Highway Funding Transferability Improvement Act aims to improve the transferability of highway funding to support more efficient and effective use of available funds. The exact details of the policy are not specified, but it suggests a potential reorganization of how highway funding is allocated and utilized.

title: Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2027
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 604.
summary: This act is a government spending bill that likely funds departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education for the year 2027. The bill likely appropriates funds for various programs and initiatives within these departments.

title: An act to provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of H. Con. Res. 14.
action: Became Public Law No: 119-21.
summary: This act addresses a reconciliation plan in accordance with Title II of House Concurrent Resolution 14. The specifics of the act are not provided due to a lack of description.

title: Small Business Technological Act of 2025
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 591.
summary: The Small Business Technological Act of 2025 aims to support and enhance technology capabilities for small businesses in the US. However, without further specifics, its exact provisions and goals remain unclear.

title: Advanced Air Mobility Coordination and Leadership Act
action: Became Public Law No: 117-203.
summary: The Advanced Air Mobility Coordination and Leadership Act aims to coordinate and provide leadership in the development and implementation of advanced air mobility technology. Its focus is on establishing a framework for safe and efficient integration of advanced air mobility systems into the national airspace.

title: First Rhode Island Regiment Congressional Gold Medal Act
action: Message on Senate action sent to the House.
summary: The First Rhode Island Regiment Congressional Gold Medal Act is a law that likely involves awarding a Congressional Gold Medal to a specific group, but no further information is available about the specifics of the award. It is presumably related to the history and service of the First Rhode Island Regiment.

title: Secure America Act
action: Became Public Law No: 119-98.
summary: The Secure America Act is a policy aimed at improving national security in the United States to protect its citizens and interests.

title: Recruiting Families Using Data Act of 2025
action: Message on Senate action sent to the House.
summary: The Recruiting Families Using Data Act of 2025 is a policy that likely deals with the use of data to recruit families for various purposes. The exact scope and intent of the policy are not specified.

title: PRESS Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The PRESS Act is a proposed law that outlines guidelines for the use and regulation of artificial intelligence systems. However, without more information, its specific details and implications are unknown.

title: Economic Relief for Specialty Crops Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: The Economic Relief for Specialty Crops Act aims to provide financial assistance to growers of specialty crops during times of economic hardship. This law likely offers funding or other forms of relief to help maintain the livelihoods of these farmers.
